title: heuchera menziesii
intro: Heuchera menziesii, also known as the alpine coral bells, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest. This plant is known for its distinctive maple-like leaves with lobed or deeply cut edges, which come in a variety of colors including red, pink, and purple. The roots of Heuchera menziesii have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Heuchera menziesii can be prepared as a tea or tincture to harness its medicinal properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory ailments.
